statement: Judge Gym V3 should adopt a strict two-tier figure architecture: a small report-grade hero set and a larger exploratory drilldown set, because the current all-in-one investigation output is too dense to function as a reader-facing report.
rationale: The current curated figures are consistently legible while several giant matrix figures remain exploratory-only due to annotation and label density.
keyAssumptions: Readers should not need zooming or local context to interpret the main report figures.
validationCriteria: A small fixed set of hero figures can summarize the core V3 findings without requiring any matrix larger than a few dozen labeled cells.
Large matrix figures are retained only in appendix/exploratory folders.
critiquePoints: Some unreadable figures may be salvageable by resizing or removing annotations rather than being demoted.
questions: Which figure classes belong in the hero set by default?
